questions.question: A right triangle has legs of length 5 and 12. If θ is the acute angle opposite the side of length 5, what is sin(θ) + cos(θ)?
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is √(25+144) = 13. Then sin(θ) = 5/13 and cos(θ) = 12/13, so sin(θ) + cos(θ) = 17/13.

questions.question: If tan(α) = 3/4 and α is in the first quadrant, find the value of 5sin(α) + 4cos(α).
questions.explanation: From tan(α) = 3/4, construct a right triangle with opposite = 3, adjacent = 4, hypotenuse = 5. Then sin(α) = 3/5 and cos(α) = 4/5, so 5(3/5) + 4(4/5) = 3 + 16/5 = 8.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(x) · sec(x) · csc(x) − tan(x).
questions.explanation: sin²(x) · sec(x) · csc(x) = sin²(x) · (1/cos(x)) · (1/sin(x)) = sin(x)/cos(x) = tan(x). So tan(x) − tan(x) = 0.

questions.question: In a circle of radius 10, a chord subtends a central angle of 60°. What is the length of the chord?
If cos(2β) = 3/5 and β is acute, what is sin(β)?
questions.explanation: Use cos(2β) = 1 − 2sin²(β). Then 3/5 = 1 − 2sin²(β), so 2sin²(β) = 2/5, giving sin²(β) = 1/5. Since β is acute, sin(β) = √5/5.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, making an angle of 70° with the ground. If the ladder is 8 meters long, how many meters up the wall does it reach? (Round to the nearest tenth.)
